generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT sensor measuring indoor temperature with metadata including tenant IDs, device IDs, and endpoint addresses for real-time data acquisition via Eclipse Hono"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance HumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT sensor measuring indoor humidity levels with metadata including tenant IDs, device IDs, and endpoint addresses for real-time data acquisition via Eclipse Hono"
    DTDFVocab:producedData HumidityDataTransmitted
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT sensor detecting occupancy status with metadata including tenant IDs, device IDs, and endpoint addresses for real-time data acquisition via Eclipse Hono"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance LightLevelSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT sensor measuring ambient light levels with metadata including tenant IDs, device IDs, and endpoint addresses for real-time data acquisition via Eclipse Hono"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LightLevelDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyConsumptionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT sensor monitoring energy consumption with metadata including tenant IDs, device IDs, and endpoint addresses for real-time data acquisition via Eclipse Hono"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance HumidityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HumiditySensor
]

instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance LightLevelDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LightLevelSensor
]

instance EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BimModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Building Information Model (BIM) serving as the semantic backbone of the Digital Twin, enriched with runtime semantics for sensor metadata and control logic"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoEnabler, EclipseDittoEnabler
]

instance SensorMetadataModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Digital representation of device metadata (tenant IDs, device IDs, endpoint addresses) extracted from BIM models for real-time synchronization"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDittoEnabler
]

instance OperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time operational data including sensor readings (temperature, humidity, occupancy, light levels, energy consumption) ingested via Eclipse Hono"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted, HumidityDataTransmitted, OccupancyDataTransmitted, LightLevelDataTransmitted, EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoEnabler, EclipseDittoEnabler
]

instance DeviceStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Normalized device states and operational metrics from Building Automation Systems (BAS) synchronized with the BIM-based Digital Twin"
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyDataTransmitted, EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDittoEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance EclipseHonoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Hono providing secure, lightweight M2M communication protocol for real-time data ingestion from IoT sensors and BAS devices"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveAnalyticsService, AutomatedCommissioningService
]

instance EclipseDittoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Ditto managing digital twin instances and their digital representations, ensuring seamless synchronization between physical and virtual states"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveAnalyticsService, AutomatedCommissioningService, CustomServiceIntegration
]

instance UnrealEngine5Enabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Unreal Engine 5 providing rich, interactive 3D visualization environments for immersive monitoring of building infrastructures"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance ModelDrivenEngineeringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-driven engineering methodologies automating the transformation of BIM models into runtime artifacts (middleware configurations, control scripts)"
    DTDFVocab:enables AutomatedCommissioningService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring and visualization service using Unreal Engine 5 for immersive 3D environments displaying operational metrics"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeDashboardInsight, InteractiveVisualizationAction
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics and optimization service using reinforcement learning for adaptive building systems (e.g., BOREALIS project for lighting optimization)"
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveFailureInsight, EnergyOptimizationAction
]

instance AutomatedCommissioningService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated commissioning and bidirectional communication service enabling real-time synchronization between virtual and physical assets (e.g., TwinLight project)"
    DTDFVocab:provides BidirectionalSynchronizationAction, AutomatedControlAction
]

instance CustomServiceIntegration : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Custom service integration framework allowing DT engineers to develop domain-specific plugins (e.g., energy management, occupant comfort optimization)"
    DTDFVocab:provides CustomPluginInsight, DomainSpecificAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeDashboardInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time dashboards visualizing operational metrics (energy use, occupant comfort) for Facility Managers"
]

instance InteractiveVisualizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D visualization of building infrastructure in Unreal Engine 5 for immersive monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance PredictiveFailureInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics identifying trends (equipment failures, energy waste) via reinforcement learning (BOREALIS project)"
]

instance EnergyOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Optimization recommendations suggesting adjustments (lighting schedules, HVAC setpoints) to improve energy efficiency"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance BidirectionalSynchronizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated synchronization between physical sensor data and virtual twin model via Eclipse Hono/Ditto"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AutomatedControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated control logic execution for Building Control Systems (BCS) components (e.g., adaptive lighting, HVAC adjustments)"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance CustomPluginInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Domain-specific insights generated by custom plugins (e.g., energy management, occupant comfort optimization)"
]

instance DomainSpecificAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Domain-specific actions executed by custom plugins (e.g., personalized comfort adjustments, regulatory compliance checks)"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance LightingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System (BCS) actuator controlling adaptive lighting systems with bidirectional communication via Eclipse Hono/Ditto"
]

instance HVACActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System (BCS) actuator managing HVAC controls with real-time adjustments enabled by the Digital Twin"
]

instance ShadingDeviceActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System (BCS) actuator controlling shading devices for energy optimization and occupant comfort"
]

instance PLCController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Programmable Logic Controller (PLC) managing building automation systems with bidirectional communication to the Digital Twin"
]

// C10: Additional Models (from dt_models_and_data)
instance EnrichedBimModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "BIM model enriched with runtime semantics including sensor metadata and control logic for Digital Twin integration"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDittoEnabler, ModelDrivenEngineeringEnabler
]

instance DigitalDeviceRepresentation : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Digital representations of devices (sensors, actuators) managed via Eclipse Ditto for real-time state tracking"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDittoEnabler
]

// C11: Additional Enablers (from tooling_and_enablers)
instance StandardizedExtensionPoints : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Standardized extension points for integrating custom services and third-party tools (e.g., reinforcement learning models for BOREALIS)"
    DTDFVocab:enables CustomServiceIntegration
]

// C6: Additional Services (from dt_services)
instance ReinforcementLearningService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Reinforcement learning service for adaptive building systems (e.g., BOREALIS project for lighting optimization)"
    DTDFVocab:provides AdaptiveControlInsight
]

// C17: Additional Insights/Actions
instance AdaptiveControlInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Adaptive control strategies generated by reinforcement learning for dynamic building optimization"
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The interactions from the virtual world to the physical world involve **control signals and commands** generated by the DT’s services (e.g., optimization algorithms, reinforcement learning models) and transmitted back to the BCS via middleware. The framework automates the generation of **runtime interfaces** for actuators and controllers, enabling the DT to adjust physical systems in real-time (e.g., dimming lights, activating HVAC systems). This is achieved through **model-to-model and model-to-text transformations** that map virtual control logic to physical execution endpoints."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twinning time scale is **real-time**, with continuous synchronization between the physical and virtual worlds. The framework supports **sub-second latency** for critical control loops (e.g., lighting adjustments) and **near-real-time updates** for broader operational data (e.g., energy consumption trends). The **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric** is used to evaluate the fidelity of synchronization, ensuring high temporal alignment between the DT and its physical counterpart."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is orchestrated as a **service-oriented architecture (SOA)**, where the DT framework acts as a central hub connecting:
  - **BIM-based design models** (static representations).
  - **Real-time operational data** (dynamic representations).
  - **Middleware services** (Eclipse Hono/Ditto for data exchange).
  - **Visualization and analytics services** (Unreal Engine 5, custom plugins).
  The constellation supports **modular integration**, allowing stakeholders (e.g., Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers) to interact with specific DT components (e.g., monitoring dashboards, control interfaces) while maintaining a unified system view."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT covers the entire life cycle of building infrastructures, including:
  1. **Design phase**: Leveraging BIM for static modeling and initial device configuration.
  2. **Construction phase**: Integrating as-built data and sensor deployments.
  3. **Operational phase**: Real-time monitoring, predictive maintenance, and adaptive control.
  4. **Refurbishment/Adaptation phase**: Dynamic updates to the DT to reflect physical modifications.
  The DT’s representation evolves from **as-designed (ideal)** to **as-manufactured** and finally to **as-operated**, ensuring alignment with the physical asset’s state throughout its life cycle."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity is evaluated using the **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric**, which quantifies the alignment between the DT and its physical counterpart. Validity considerations include:
  - **Verification**: Ensuring the DT’s models and data accurately reflect the physical system (e.g., via sensor calibration and model validation).
  - **Validation**: Testing the DT’s services in real-world scenarios (e.g., Living Lab case studies).
  - **Uncertainty management**: Addressing data gaps or inconsistencies through **semantic normalization** and **real-time error correction**.
  The framework also incorporates **quality assurance** mechanisms to detect and resolve **semantic drift** (divergence between digital and physical states) over time."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection between the physical twin (PT) and digital twin (DT) relies on:
  - **Eclipse Hono**: Provides **MQTT/AMQP** protocols for secure, lightweight data exchange between IoT devices and the DT.
  - **Eclipse Ditto**: Manages **digital twin instances** and their **digital twins** (DTs) via **HTTP/REST APIs** for state synchronization.
  - **Bidirectional communication**: Enabled through **model-to-model transformations** that map BIM elements to runtime endpoints (e.g., sensor IDs to middleware topics).
  The architecture supports **scalable network topologies**, including **edge computing** for low-latency control loops."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is hosted in a **modular, service-oriented deployment architecture**, with components distributed across:
  - **Cloud/edge servers**: For middleware (Hono/Ditto) and analytics services.
  - **Local BIM environments**: For design-time modeling and initial configuration.
  - **Unreal Engine 5**: For visualization, deployed on high-performance workstations or cloud-based rendering platforms.
  The framework supports **containerized deployment** (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes) for scalability and **hybrid cloud-edge setups** to optimize latency for real-time services."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration involves exchanging information with **external systems**, including:
  - **Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems**: For financial and asset management data.
  - **Building Energy Management Systems (BEMS)**: For cross-platform energy optimization.
  - **Other Digital Twins**: Via standardized APIs (e.g., Ditto’s **Thing Description** format) for interoperability.
  The framework supports **semantic interoperability** through **BIM-based ontologies** and **middleware normalization**, ensuring seamless data exchange across heterogeneous systems."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "Data ownership is governed by **stakeholder-specific roles** (e.g., Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers) and **BIM-based access controls**. Privacy considerations include:
  - **Role-based access**: Restricting data visibility to authorized users (e.g., DT Engineers for service development).
  - **GDPR compliance**: Anonymizing occupant data (e.g., sensor readings) where required.
  - **Secure data pipelines**: Encrypting data in transit (e.g., TLS for Hono/Ditto) and at rest.
  The framework does not explicitly address **PT owner vs. DT service provider** ownership conflicts but assumes alignment with **AECO industry standards** (e.g., ISO 19650)."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The DT adheres to:
  - **BIM standards**: IFC (Industry Foundation Classes) for model exchange and **IDM (Information Delivery Manual)** for data structuring.
  - **IoT standards**: Eclipse Hono’s **OMA LWM2M** and **MQTT** for device communication.
  - **Digital Twin standards**: Eclipse Ditto’s **Thing Description** format for interoperability.
  - **Model-driven engineering (MDE) standards**: For automated transformations (e.g., **MOF/QVT** for model-to-model mappings).
  The framework also defines **custom extension points** to accommodate domain-specific standards (e.g., lighting control protocols in TwinLight)."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security measures include:
  - **Authentication/Authorization**: Role-based access control (RBAC) for middleware and visualization tools.
  - **Data encryption**: TLS for Hono/Ditto communication and **secure storage** for sensitive data.
  - **Anomaly detection**: Monitoring for unauthorized access or data tampering.
  Safety considerations address:
  - **Remote operation limits**: Restricting DT-controlled actions to **pre-approved scenarios** (e.g., lighting adjustments) to prevent physical hazards.
  - **Fail-safes**: Defaulting to manual control or predefined states in case of system failures.
  The framework does not explicitly detail **human safety protocols** for critical operations (e.g., HVAC shutdowns) but assumes compliance with **AECO safety regulations** (e.g., ISO 55000 for asset management)."
]
